value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Visitors defined as non-residents will be eligible to use Japan’s new tax refund system starting November 1, 2026.
Some countries have agreements with Japan allowing residents to stay up to 6 months without a visa; if staying more than 90 days, an extension must be applied for at the Immigration Services Agency.
Foreign tourists who are in Japan for less than 6 months are eligible for consumption tax exemption on articles purchased at specified tax-free shops, provided the articles are taken out of Japan.
Visit Japan Web prompts users to check whether or not they need a visa for Japan before registering their travel plans.
Foreign visitors in Japan can access emergency medical assistance services including medical referrals, emergency patient transports, hospital arrangements, and interpretation services.

Foreign transaction fees are charges applied by credit card issuers when you make purchases or cash withdrawals in a currency different from the card’s currency; these fees may include currency conversion fees, foreign transaction fee percentages, and fixed transaction fees.
Local residents pay 1,000 yen; foreign tourists may be charged 2,500 yen
Junguria Okinawa theme park will charge different entry prices for foreigners and Japanese residents
All-you-can-eat seafood restaurant Tamatebako in Shibuya charges different prices for residents and tourists
Tokyo Shibuya Sky Admission and 24 Hour Subway Ticket costs $44
teamLab Forest Fukuoka Immersive Art Experience Ticket costs $27
The average food cost in Japan is $30–50 per day for most travelers in 2026.

The average cost of a hotel in Japan varies depending on location, time of year, star rating, and amenities; budget options include hostels, guesthouses, and capsule hotels which offer more affordable rates than traditional hotels.
Single-entry visa fee is $20.00
Using an ambulance in Japan is free of charge, even for international visitors
Duty-free allowance for general goods is up to 200,000 yen; items exceeding this limit are subject to a 15% duty plus a 10% consumption tax
There is no fee for entering Japan via Visit Japan Web; any $60 charge pop-up is from scam websites.
Purchases of 1,000,000 JPY (tax-exclusive) or more will still be subject to detailed product registration to support tax-free handling and customs verification procedures.
Starting July 1, 2026, Japan will raise its departure tax from JPY 1,000 (USD 6.47) to JPY 3,000 (USD 19.41) per person for travellers leaving by air or sea.
The Japan Rail Pass is available only for non-Japanese nationals visiting Japan on a tourist visa (Temporary Visitor status).
Suica cards are not free and can be purchased at ticket vending machines in major JR stations in Eastern Japan.
Travelers should review coverage maps during trip planning and pre-arrange internet access such as Japan eSIM plans to support multi-city travel and quick navigation through transport systems.

To use a SIM card in Japan, you need to understand your visa status as it affects eligibility and required documentation. You must prepare your residence card showing your Japanese address. Setting up a Japanese bank account is essential for postpaid contracts and makes payments easier. Prioritize providers with strong English support for a smooth experience.
Many hotels in Japan, especially traditional ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.
Ryokan inns are located throughout Japan, including places like Kawaguchiko and Kyoto

In large cities such as Tokyo, there is no pressing need to include meals with your accommodation because you have many food options nearby, but in rural or regional areas, you should check what eating options are available outside your guesthouse.
Travelers should review coverage maps during trip planning to ensure reliable mobile internet access in Japan.
Tourists can buy SIM cards at Travelex Counter, Level B1, Narita Airport Terminal 2 Station, SKYLINER & KEISEI INFORMATION, and at 2nd Floor, Hiroshima Airport International Terminal Building.
Mobile broadband devices including prepaid Pocket WiFi routers with 100GB/365 days no contract plans are available for purchase on Amazon.co.jp
Kinmata, Kyoto, Japan
Typical dinner time in Japan generally falls between 6:00 p.m. and 8:00 p.m., though this can vary depending on individual household routines and work or school schedules.

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, requiring the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
The Suica card initially costs ¥1000, of which ¥500 is credit and ¥500 is a refundable deposit
For popular restaurants in Japan, it’s generally a good idea to make a reservation in advance, unless the restaurant does not accept bookings.
Some stores inside and around train stations, many konbini, vending machines, and other stores accept payment with prepaid IC cards such as Suica and PASMO in Japan.
Visa and Mastercard credit cards are widely accepted in Japan for purchases, payments, and ATM withdrawals.
Japan uses many international payment methods such as Apple Pay and Google Pay, and domestic payment methods like PayPay are popular for QR code payments.
IC travel cards available for tourists do not require a deposit, but physical IC travel cards purchased at station ticket counters or automatic ticket vending machines require a deposit.

Residents with bank accounts in Japan can apply for a debit card, but may need to select payment options for online shopping when setting up the card. Seven Bank ATMs are available in 7-Eleven stores. PayPay is a mobile payment service for cashless smartphone transactions. English-friendly alternatives like Revolut and Wise offer debit cards usable in Japan. Visa and Mastercard cards are widely accepted for purchases, payments, and ATM withdrawals.
Most arrests of U.S. citizens in Japan are for drug-related offenses.

Japanese convenience stores offer a huge variety of fresh, high-quality food including onigiri, sushi, sandwiches, salads, bento boxes, noodle dishes, single-serving fruit, baked goods, beverages, candy, and snacks at budget-friendly prices.
The validity period of a Japan Rail Pass is the consecutive 7, 14, or 21 days beginning on the date the pass can actually be first used.

A Suica card is valid for 10 years if used; if not used for 10 years, it becomes invalid.
Purchase the Japan Rail Pass on the official Japan Rail Pass Reservation website using your passport and credit card.
The Japan Rail Pass exchange voucher is valid for 90 days after issue date
